title: When will you release v4 binaries (ie v4.0.2) ?
body:
When will you release v4 binaries (ie v4.0.2) to this repository, in my case for Ubuntu 21.10

`https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/podman`

Or am I looking in the wrong place?

```
REF Your documentation:
https://podman.io/getting-started/installation
CAUTION: On Ubuntu 20.10 and newer, we highly recommend you use Buildah, Podman and Skopeo ONLY from EITHER the Kubic repo OR the official Ubuntu repos
```

comments.body:
Possibly never:

* https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13302

See also:

https://packages.ubuntu.com/impish/podman (3.2.1)

Possibly needs an updated blog post about it: https://podman.io/blogs/2021/03/02/podman-support-for-older-distros.html

As far as I know, it is only in Debian "[experimental](https://packages.debian.org/experimental/podman)" (4.0.1). It is not in Debian "sid" (3.4.4), and not in Ubuntu 22.04 LTS (3.4.4).
I will update the podman docs.

> Possibly needs an updated blog post about it: https://podman.io/blogs/2021/03/02/podman-support-for-older-distros.html

@afbjorklund wip blog post for v4.0 is happening at https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13310

As far as I know, the system packages will stay at the current versions:

Ubuntu | Podman | Kubic
--- | --- | ----
20.04 | --- | (3.4.2)
20.10 | --- | (3.4.2)
21.04 | 3.0 |  (3.4.2)
21.10 | 3.2 | ----
22.04 | 3.4 | ----


And then it is up to third parties, to supply the packaging for Podman 4.

Basically it will only be available in Fedora 36, possibly backported to RHEL.
